  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening an abstract often means reforging the gap statement and contribution claim into sharper form; the existing framing of what SelfProbe uniquely solves is a plausible target for that kind of work.",
    "R1.4": "Three explicit 'first' novelty claims appear in the text; making the piece 'stronger' naturally includes scrutinizing and sharpening how the delta from prior introspection/calibration work is characterized.",
    "R1.6": "The most direct reading of 'make this stronger' for a research abstract is improving rhetorical momentum, sentence-level clarity, and paragraph cohesion — prose polish is load-bearing here."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By 'stronger,' do you primarily mean improving prose quality and rhetorical force (sentence/paragraph polish), or do you want the thesis and contribution claims restructured — e.g., reframing what problem SelfProbe is positioned as solving or how novelty is argued?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening an abstract often means reforging the gap statement and contribution claim into sharper form; the existing framing of what SelfProbe uniquely solves is a plausible target for that kind of work.
rationale.R1.4: Three explicit 'first' novelty claims appear in the text; making the piece 'stronger' naturally includes scrutinizing and sharpening how the delta from prior introspection/calibration work is characterized.
rationale.R1.6: The most direct reading of 'make this stronger' for a research abstract is improving rhetorical momentum, sentence-level clarity, and paragraph cohesion — prose polish is load-bearing here.

parsed.clarification_question: By 'stronger,' do you primarily mean improving prose quality and rhetorical force (sentence/paragraph polish), or do you want the thesis and contribution claims restructured — e.g., reframing what problem SelfProbe is positioned as solving or how novelty is argued?
